title: Tej Daruwalla

meta.role: Executive Vice President, Data Solutions & Analytics
meta.bio:
Tej is a transformational leader at the intersection of emerging technology and strategic business growth. With more than 20 years of experience across the healthcare and consumer packaged goods industries, she helps organizations harness data, AI, and innovation to drive measurable business impact. Her expertise spans enterprise-wide data and AI strategy, translating complex information into practical solutions that create value for both businesses and consumers. Known for her thoughtful, problem-solving approach, Tej believes technology is most powerful when it simplifies complexity and unlocks new possibilities for human-centered industries.

She holds a bachelor’s degree from the University of Mumbai and an MBA from Penn State University (#WeAre, #GoSteelers).

When Tej isn’t leading global teams or exploring the evolving frontier of Agentic AI, Tej enjoys spending time outdoors, including hiking through the Great Smoky Mountains with her Golden Retriever, Daisy.
meta.fun_fact: Hailing from northern India,Tej is a quarter-Italian hand-talker who speaks three languages— ironically, Italian is not one of them.

title: Shelly Fifolt

meta.bio:
Shelly is a seasoned client service and strategy leader with more than 20 years of experience in life sciences and deep expertise in the pharmaceutical industry. She leads agency-client relationships with a focus on high-touch, white-glove service, ensuring clients feel prioritized, supported, and heard while helping drive strong business outcomes.

Her experience spans the full commercial lifecycle, from disease education, access strategy, and stakeholder engagement through brand planning and execution. With a strong understanding of complex healthcare landscapes and evolving client needs, Shelly brings clarity, insight, and steady leadership to every stage of the work.

Drawing on both client-side and partner perspectives, Shelly builds trusted relationships, simplifies complexity and helps clients navigate challenges with confidence. She is known for her thoughtful approach, strong collaboration, and commitment to delivering an exceptional client experience.

title: Julie Brecht

meta.bio:
Julie Brecht brings more than 20 years of experience in pharmaceutical marketing to lead with strategy, structure, and a strong commitment to client service. With deep expertise in client relationship management and agency operations, she helps guide complex programs with clarity, collaboration, and operational excellence.

Julie has a strong focus in oncology, and has partnered with leading pharmaceutical brands across a range of disease areas, including rare and liver cancers, metastatic breast cancer, prostate cancer and small cell lung cancer. She is known for bringing insight and steady leadership to teams and clients alike, helping drive meaningful engagement with clinicians, advocates, and patients.

Julie’s ability to connect the dots across strategy, service, and execution makes her a trusted partner in delivering work that is both effective and impactful.
meta.fun_fact: Julie trips over her own feet, but her kids more than make up for it—her son was part of Ohio State’s 2024 National Championship football team, and her daughter plays Division I soccer at Butler.

title: Bill Werbaneth

meta.bio:
Bill has spent most of his career in advertising, bringing deep pharmaceutical experience and a sharp understanding of what resonates with both patients and prescribers. His work has spanned blockbuster therapies and rare-disease treatments alike, giving him a broad perspective on how meaningful communications can help people better understand complex healthcare decisions.

Known for his curiosity and hands-on approach, Bill loves digging into the latest research and translating science into clear, compelling copy. He has a talent for making highly technical information feel accessible, relevant, and human.

Originally from Pittsburgh, Bill has lived in Brooklyn, London, and Springfield, New Jersey. He and his family now call northern New Jersey home, a place he’ll tell you is far more beautiful and bucolic than most people expect.
meta.fun_fact: Bill once appeared as a contestant on VH1’s Rock & Roll Jeopardy!, where host Jeff Probst spent the breaks between rounds promoting his “new show” to the audience. By the time Bill’s taping wrapped on the West Coast, the premiere episode of Survivor was already airing back East.

title: Marion Coda

meta.bio:
Marion brings 18 years of advertising experience to BPD, with a career shaped by living and working in five states and collaborating with talented teams across the country. Over the years, Marion has earned Addy Awards, had work featured in art shows, and received IMDb credit for creating the opening sequence for the Aqua Teen Hunger Force movie—one of the more memorable milestones in a creative career defined by range, curiosity, and craft. At BPD, where she has spent the past nine years, Marion is known for pairing strong design instincts with a genuine commitment to helping others succeed. She believes great creative does more than capture attention—it helps ideas grow, goals move forward, and people reach their full potential.

Outside of BPD, Marion is also a working artist whose paintings draw inspiration from nature, the human body, and the organic forms that shape the visual world. She is the founder of Rays of Sunshine Bus, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit serving the local tri-state recovery community through art and design, helping raise awareness, reduce stigma around addiction and alcoholism, and create space for people in recovery to build confidence and reconnect with their sense of value. When she is not working or painting, Marion enjoys life at her historic farmhouse just minutes from the beach. A proud homebody who also loves hiking and the outdoors, she is a devoted dog mom who keeps family, creativity, and community at the center of everything she does.

title: Krissy Onore

meta.bio:
Krissy is an Account Director at BPD with more than 25 years of marketing experience and a career built on versatility, curiosity, and follow-through. A Penn State graduate, she’s held roles across the full spectrum of the industry—from advertising and promotions to media planning, project management, and market research operations—giving her a well-rounded perspective on what it takes to move work from insight to impact.

Known for her collaborative style, Krissy partners closely with internal teams to translate strategy into smart, effective execution that helps clients reach their business goals while strengthening the relationships that make great work possible. She believes growth comes from saying “yes” to new experiences because you never know where you’ll discover your next passion or perspective.

Outside of work, Krissy and her husband, Dave, stay busy keeping up with their three energetic sons and two dogs. Summers often mean family time in the Outer Banks with her parents, along with adventures that may include Sesame Place, LEGOLAND, and whatever is next on the family calendar.

title: Halen Van Dang

meta.bio:
Halen is a Creative Director at BPD with more than 20 years of experience leading work across pharmaceutical, consumer, and experiential marketing. He brings a rare blend of creative instinct and strategic discipline—building ideas rooted not in genre or trend, but in human reaction. Deeply attuned to how people perceive, internalize, and respond to information, Halen approaches every assignment through strategy and storytelling, using design as a tool to create work that resonates and performs.

A digital specialist at heart, Halen is an expert in translating complex messages into intuitive, engaging experiences. With a master’s degree in Interactive Design and Media from Jefferson University and a career shaped by digitally focused agencies, he brings deep knowledge of user experience, design, and development—helping teams create smart, connected work across channels.

Throughout his career, Halen’s HCP and DTC campaigns have earned top industry recognition, spanning multiple platforms and therapeutic areas including oncology, men’s and women’s health, respiratory, pain, and rare disease.
meta.fun_fact: Halen was named after the band Van Halen.

title: Nicole Ficarrotta

meta.bio:
Nicole is an Account Supervisor at BPD with 10 years of experience supporting CPG and pharmaceutical brands, where she’s built a career around strong client relationships and high-touch service. A graduate of the University of Hartford, Nicole began her career in New York City, where she sharpened her passion for client services and learned what it takes to keep fast-moving workstreams on track.

Nicole is known for showing up as a true extension of her clients’ teams—anticipating needs, solving problems early, and bringing calm clarity to complex projects. She prides herself on being a trusted partner to both her clients and her coworkers, pairing meticulous organization with a collaborative, can-do approach that helps teams deliver their best work.

title: Robert Giorgio

meta.bio:
Rob brings curiosity and creativity to everything he builds—an instinct that started early with cartoons, comic books like Spawn, and a deep appreciation for ’90s album art. He earned his degree in graphic design from Drexel University, shaping the visual storytelling foundation that still informs his work today.

Now working at the intersection of science and creativity, Rob thrives in healthcare marketing for its rare blend of strategic rigor and human impact. He enjoys untangling complex challenges and turning them into smart, effective campaigns that break through and deliver results. His experience spans agencies, nonprofits, and corporate environments, giving him a versatile perspective and a steady focus on what works.

Outside the office, Rob creates abstract art and has shown his work in coffee shops and galleries. Grounded by his faith and a love of philosophy, he approaches life with thoughtful curiosity and intention. A proud husband and father of three, he can usually be found hiking, cheering from the sidelines at his kids’ games, or pushing himself at CrossFit.
meta.fun_fact: As a kid, Rob memorized the TV Guide—and can still recall ’90s movie star ratings.

title: Patrick Tom

meta.bio:
Patrick Tom is the Executive Creative Director at BPD, where he brings a global perspective shaped by a career that spans four countries and some of the world’s most iconic brands. A true citizen of the world, Patrick has built and led creative teams across continents, uniting diverse talent through a shared passion for storytelling and innovation.

Patrick’s experience bridges both brand and agency worlds. He has held in-house roles at Disney and creative leadership positions with top agencies including TBWA\Worldwide, Publicis Worldwide, Saatchi & Saatchi Worldwide, and Venables Bell & Partners. He is also an accomplished commercial film director and founder of Liberum13, a creative content studio known for its cinematic storytelling.

His work has been recognized internationally at Cannes Lions, The One Show, The Webby Awards, Effies, New York Festivals, Lürzer’s Archive, Communication Arts, Graphis, MM+M Awards, Fierce Pharma Awards, PM360, and more. Patrick was also ranked the #32 director in the world by BestAdsOnTV.com.

A respected voice in the creative community, Patrick has served on juries for global award shows including Cannes Lions, New York Festivals, Dubai Lynx, Spikes Asia, Effies, MadStars, Tambuli Awards, Tomorrow Awards, Cristal Festival, The Singapore Outdoor Advertising Awards, and Premios Volcan Festival.

While his professional accolades are extensive, Patrick cites his most memorable creative achievement as earning a top ten hit in Finland with his former band. Outside of work, he’s a proud dad and husband, a surfer and skater, part-time bass player, animal rescuer, and self-described Type 9 peacemaker.
meta.fun_fact: Patrick's old band once had a top ten hit in Finland.

meta.bio:
As Senior Vice President of Communications at BPD, Nicole James leads the firm’s communications practice, helping healthcare organizations build trust, protect reputations and amplify their leadership on a national and global stage. A trusted counselor to senior executives, she guides health systems and academic medical centers in defining their voice, advancing thought leadership, and engaging the audiences who matter most, from policymakers and physicians to patients and communities.

He helps healthcare brands turn data, technology and AI into measurably better digital user experiences. He partners with hospitals and health systems to further leverage their existing martech, building roadmaps that drive digital engagement and measurable financial impact. A self described advocate of the digital user experience, he believes the ideal digital experience benefits both the end user and organization. He also supports BPD's development of new internal offerings across UX, AI and CRM, and the frameworks that guide clients as they scale.

With five years at BPD across healthcare, nursing and pharma, and prior experience in B2B, life sciences and biotech. Tyler brings a cross-industry lens to every engagement. He's known for cutting through the noise of competing technologies and AI hype to help marketers quantify impact and invest where it counts: the end-user. Outside of work, you'll find him outdoors whenever possible with his family and dog Madge!
meta.fun_fact: In college Tyler trained horses at a farm and gave riding lessons as a side job.

title: Sarah Serafini

meta.bio: Sarah is SVP, Creative Operations at BPD and an award-winning production executive with two decades of experience leading the development and execution of marketing campaigns, commercials, premium television, film, and digital content. title: Drew Marlatt

meta.bio:
With a career built on delivering custom data-driven solutions across industries from healthcare and life sciences to financial services and defense, Drew now serves as Vice President of Data Products and Technology at BPD. He thrives on solving complex problems with analytics, statistics, machine learning, and generative AI—always focused on creating the simplest, most effective solutions to generate insight and improve efficiency. As an industry thought leader on data privacy and responsible AI, Drew believes cutting-edge technology must be applied with care to build trust and ensure fairness for both users and society. His approach blends innovation with responsibility, ensuring BPD’s data products deliver measurable value while upholding the highest ethical standards.

Originally from rural Oklahoma, Drew has made Madison, Wisconsin, home with his family. Outside of work, he enjoys hiking, reading, and experimenting in the kitchen, where he crafts and bottles small-batch fermented hot sauces—each with a signature pun-filled name.
meta.fun_fact: Drew and his spouse, Lauren, first met while working on an oil rig in South Texas.

title: Siva Gangadharan

meta.bio:
Siva moved to the United States to pursue a Master's degree in Engineering and began his career as an ETL Consultant in the Northeast. Siva has lived in Nashville, TN for the past 15 years, and has built a strong career in data engineering and leadership. As a data engineering professional, Siva excels in developing data strategies, leading high-performing teams, and delivering impactful results. He is passionate about mentoring engineers and working with cross-functional teams to implement strategic data initiatives. Siva's interest in healthcare drives him to leverage technology to improve patient outcomes, focusing on data quality, governance, and modernizing tech stacks. Siva also manages vendor relationships, optimizes costs, and streamlines processes to maximize budget efficiency and scalability.

Originally from southern India, Siva now lives in Franklin, TN, with his spouse and two kids. In his free time, he enjoys running, biking, and playing ball with his son, and is actively involved in community volunteering.
meta.fun_fact: Siva is a foodie with a sweet tooth and can’t resist a good dessert!

Her journey started at the Newhouse School at Syracuse University, where a quick pivot from broadcast journalism to advertising sparked a lifelong love of strategic storytelling—one that blends culture, creativity, and just the right amount of irreverence.

She’s spent her career at top NYC agencies crafting integrated campaigns for brands big and small, always with an eye toward insight and impact (and the occasional bold creative ask—like convincing a teenage boy to shave his head to look like a lizard). When she’s not concepting campaigns, Jess can be found hiking, traveling, enjoying dessert, or curating her home with vintage furniture that once belonged to someone else’s grandparents.
funFact: Jessica has very unique thumbs- she will most likely check out your thumbs to see if you are her thumb twin.

meta.fun_fact: Alejo is a big fan of motorcycles and vintage cars.
